theatres.name: Iran / Strait of Hormuz
theatres.summary: Day 24 of US-Israel air campaign. War entering fourth week with nuclear-facility tit-for-tat escalation. US struck Natanz nuclear enrichment complex again with bunker busters (Mar 22); Iran retaliated with missiles on Dimona and Arad, wounding 180+ near Israel's nuclear research center — first successful penetration of Israeli air defenses in that area. Iran fired 2 intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia (4,000km), revealing previously unknown long-range capability; both failed to hit base. Trump's 48-hour Hormuz ultimatum (issued Mar 21) expires today; Iran says Hormuz will be 'completely closed' if power plants hit. Israel struck Tehran again Mar 23. Trump-Xi summit postponed indefinitely (was Mar 31-Apr 2); admin says no summit until Iran war ends. USS Tripoli ARG + 31st MEU expected to arrive in theatre Mar 23-27. CENTCOM: ~50,000 US troops now in Middle East. UK agreed to allow US use of British bases for Hormuz strikes. Trump said he's considering 'winding down' but then issued ultimatum. Houthis still NOT resuming Red Sea attacks. HRANA documented 3,114 deaths in Iran (higher than Health Ministry's 1,444).

theatres.name: Russia-Ukraine War
theatres.summary: Russia holds ~20% of Ukraine. 161 combat engagements recorded on Mar 21 alone. Ukraine has cleared 400 km² since counteroffensive began; February 2026 first net territorial gain since 2024. Peace talks partially resumed: US-Ukraine bilateral talks held in Miami Mar 21-22 (Witkoff, Kushner, Umerov, Budanov, Kyslytsia, Arakhamia) — described as 'constructive' but Russia was NOT present. Abu Dhabi trilateral talks still postponed. Russia advancing on Kostiantynivka and pushing toward Kramatorsk/Sloviansk. Iran war continues to divert international attention and boost Russian war economy via energy prices.

theatres.name: Latin America — Venezuela / Cuba / Colombia
theatres.summary: Maduro trial hearing set for March 26 — prosecutors rejected defense motion to dismiss charges. Rodriguez acting president — oil reform, amnesty. US recognized Rodriguez Mar 11. Cuba in energy crisis (90% fuel cutoff). Secret US-Cuba talks ongoing. Colombia in economic emergency; peace process collapsed.

theatres.name: US-China Trade
theatres.summary: Trump-Xi summit POSTPONED INDEFINITELY — was scheduled Mar 31-Apr 2 in Beijing. Trump said he wants to stay in Washington for Iran war; admin says no summit until war ends. Trump initially said 'reset' to mid-May, but Political Wire reports summit on hold until war ends. Paris trade talks (Mar 15-16) were 'very good' per Bessent. US intelligence assesses China NOT planning Taiwan invasion in 2027 but is closely studying Iran war for lessons. China's military studying US-Israeli operations against Iran per Bloomberg. Taiwan faces Mar 15 deadline to sign three US arms packages. Section 301 investigations ongoing. China's 2026 defense budget up 7% to ~$278B.

theatres.name: East Asia — DPRK / Korean Peninsula
theatres.summary: North Korea fired 10+ ballistic missiles into East Sea on Mar 14 (~350km range) during Freedom Shield exercises (Mar 9-19). KN-25 600mm multiple rocket launcher system test. Kim Yo Jong warned of 'unimaginably terrible consequences.' Third missile test in March per Japan. No further launches since Mar 14. Russia-DPRK alliance deepened; 12,000 DPRK troops in Russia.

metrics.name: US Military KIA (Iran theatre)
metrics.change: 7 killed by enemy fire, 6 in Iraq plane crash

metrics.name: Iran Civilian Casualties
metrics.change: HRANA documents 1,354 civilians, 1,138 military, 622 unclassified; Health Min. figure likely undercount

metrics.name: Israel Casualties (from Iran)
metrics.change: +180 wounded in Dimona/Arad strikes (Mar 21-22); air defense failure acknowledged

metrics.name: Lebanon Casualties (since Mar 2)
metrics.change: Israel ground ops ongoing since Mar 16; 2 IDF soldiers confirmed killed

metrics.name: UAE Casualties
metrics.change: 314 BMs + 15 CMs + 1,672 drones fired at UAE as of Mar 17

metrics.name: Qatar Gas Production
metrics.change: Iran hit Ras Laffan Mar 19 in retaliation for South Pars strike

metrics.name: Ukraine Territory Occupied
metrics.change: Ukraine cleared 400 km² in counteroffensive; 161 engagements/day

metrics.name: Cuba Fuel Imports
metrics.change: no shipments in 3 months per Diaz-Canel

ground_invasion_tracker.troop_count_in_theatre: ~50,000 (CENTCOM confirmed figure; includes 3 carrier strike groups, Tripoli ARG/31st MEU arriving imminently)
key_developments: CENTCOM confirms ~50,000 US troops now in Middle East (USNI News, Mar 23)
USS Tripoli ARG + 31st MEU expected to arrive in theatre Mar 23-27 — imminent
Tripoli ESG includes USS Tripoli, guided-missile cruiser USS Robert Smalls, guided-missile destroyer USS Rafael Peralta
CRITICAL: Axios reports Trump considering occupation or blockade of Kharg Island (Mar 20)
CRITICAL: Pentagon has drawn up detailed plans for ground operations in Iran (Times of Israel, CBS, Mar 20)
Additional 2,500 Marines ordered to Middle East (Military.com, Mar 20)
UK agreed to allow US use of British military bases for Hormuz strikes (Mar 21)
Trump says considering 'winding down' (Mar 21) but then issued 48-hour ultimatum (also Mar 21) — contradictory signals
Trump: 'not putting troops anywhere' but added 'If I were, I certainly wouldn't tell you'
Israel ground operations in southern Lebanon ongoing since Mar 16 — 2 IDF soldiers confirmed killed
22 countries signaled 'readiness to contribute' to Hormuz reopening (Mar 19 joint statement)
48-hour ultimatum deadline expires today Mar 23 — if Iran does not comply, escalation to power plant strikes likely
No draft or conscription legislation introduced in US

kharg_island.naval_activity: US bombed 90+ military targets on Kharg Island Mar 13 (runway, naval base, air defenses, mine storage). Oil infrastructure deliberately spared. Trump considering occupation or blockade per Axios (Mar 20). USS Tripoli ARG arriving imminently with 2,200 Marines — could be the force used for island seizure.
kharg_island.notes: Kharg Island handles ~90% of Iran's oil exports. Jiang predicted US seizure in multiple lectures. US has now struck the island militarily and is actively considering ground occupation — this is the closest any Jiang prediction about ground operations has come to materializing. However, island seizure ≠ mainland invasion. A Kharg operation would be a limited naval/amphibious action, not the 500K-2M troop ground war Jiang described. The 48-hour ultimatum expiring today could trigger the decision point on Kharg occupation.

marine_movements: USS Tripoli (LHA-7) + USS Robert Smalls (CG) + USS Rafael Peralta (DDG) + 31st MEU (2,200 Marines) — expected to arrive Mar 23-27
Additional 2,500 Marines ordered to region (Military.com, Mar 20)
Total Marine force heading to theatre: ~4,700
31st MEU includes F-35B Lightning II, MV-22B Ospreys, MH-60S Seahawks
Defense experts warn of Indo-Pacific gap as Marines deploy from Japan to Middle East (Stars and Stripes, Mar 19)
CENTCOM requested additional forces for 'more options for military operations against Iran'
82nd Airborne also mentioned in Pentagon planning per Defence Security Asia
ground_invasion_tracker.draft_indicators: None. No legislation introduced. No Selective Service activation. No public discussion at Pentagon or White House level.
ground_invasion_tracker.assessment: Status remains 'staging' but approaching a critical decision point. The 48-hour ultimatum expires today (Mar 23); if Trump follows through on power plant strikes and Iran retaliates by fully closing Hormuz and hitting Gulf infrastructure, the pressure for ground operations — particularly Kharg Island seizure — intensifies sharply. CENTCOM now confirms ~50,000 troops in theatre. USS Tripoli arriving imminently with Marines configured for amphibious assault. However, Trump's contradictory signals ('winding down' vs. ultimatum) suggest internal debate. Still far from Jiang's 500K-2M troops/draft scenario — but the escalation ladder is tightening with each day the ultimatum goes unanswered.

daily_entries.summary: Nuclear-facility tit-for-tat escalation marks new phase of war. US struck Iran's Natanz nuclear enrichment complex again with bunker busters (Mar 22); Iran retaliated with missiles on Dimona and Arad near Israel's Negev Nuclear Research Center, wounding 180+ — first successful penetration of Israeli air defenses in that area. Israeli military acknowledged interceptors 'failed to hit the threats.' IAEA says no radiation damage at either site. Israel struck Tehran again on Mar 23 with explosions in east of city. Iran intercepted a US-Israeli armed drone over Tehran.
details: US struck Natanz with bunker busters on Mar 22; IDF denied Israeli involvement
Iran framed Dimona/Arad strikes as 'response' to Natanz attack — nuclear tit-for-tat
116 wounded in Arad (7 serious), 64 wounded in Dimona (1 serious) — 180 total
Israeli firefighters: 'interceptors were launched that failed to hit the threats, resulting in two direct hits by ballistic missiles with warheads weighing hundreds of kilograms'
IAEA: no abnormal radiation levels detected at either Dimona or Natanz
Iran's Tasnim news agency: armed drone intercepted over Tehran airspace
Israel launched new strikes on east Tehran on Mar 23
daily_entries.prediction_impact: The nuclear-facility tit-for-tat represents a dangerous escalation — both sides are now targeting each other's nuclear infrastructure. Israeli air defense failure at Dimona is significant; if Iranian missiles can penetrate defenses near Israel's most sensitive nuclear site, the calculus for continued escalation changes.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, CBS News, NPR, BusinessToday, Euronews, Military.com, The Week, IAEA

daily_entries.summary: Iran fired 2 intermediate-range ballistic missiles at Diego Garcia, the joint US-UK military base in the Indian Ocean ~4,000km from Iran. One malfunctioned in flight, one was intercepted. No damage to base. UK confirmed the attack and condemned it as 'reckless.' This reveals Iran possesses 4,000km+ missile capability — previously assessed at 2,000km maximum. UK agreed to allow US to use British military bases for Hormuz strikes.
details: Diego Garcia is ~2,500 miles (4,000km) from Iran — double previously known Iranian missile range
Base can host B-2 stealth bombers; strategic staging area for US operations
UK MoD condemned 'reckless attacks'; confirmed both missiles failed to reach target
Analysts: 'These missiles mean Iran has 4,000km-plus ballistic missiles — not revealed before'
UK decision to allow US use of British bases for Hormuz operations came same day
Diego Garcia strike means European capitals are theoretically within Iranian missile range
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Iran's demonstrated 4,000km missile range is a major intelligence surprise. If Iran can strike Diego Garcia, it can reach parts of southern Europe. This fundamentally changes the threat calculus for European nations and could accelerate European defense spending — supporting Jiang's broader thesis about European rearmament.
daily_entries.source: Bloomberg, Al Jazeera, CNN, CNBC, Euronews, ITV News, Military.com, Fox News

daily_entries.summary: Trump's 48-hour ultimatum on Hormuz expires today (Mar 23). Iran 'unswayed' per NBC — no compliance expected. Iran's IRGC says if power plants hit, Hormuz will be 'completely closed' and won't reopen until plants are rebuilt. USS Tripoli ARG + 31st MEU expected to arrive in theatre Mar 23-27. CENTCOM confirms ~50,000 US troops now in Middle East. Trump gave contradictory signals: said considering 'winding down' (Mar 21) but then issued ultimatum same day. 82nd Airborne mentioned in Pentagon ground operation plans.
details: 48-hour ultimatum issued Saturday night Mar 21 — deadline approximately Monday Mar 23
NBC: Iran 'unswayed by Trump's 48-hour deadline and threats to obliterate energy infrastructure'
Iran IRGC: 'Strait of Hormuz will be completely closed, and it will not be reopened until our destroyed power plants are rebuilt'
CENTCOM: ~50,000 US troops in Middle East theatre
USS Tripoli ESG arriving imminently — composition: Tripoli (LHA-7), Robert Smalls (CG), Rafael Peralta (DDG), 31st MEU
Defence Security Asia: Pentagon plans include 82nd Airborne in addition to Marine units
Trump on 'winding down': told reporters considering it, but ultimatum contradicts
Admin telling foreign officials: Trump-Xi summit won't happen until Iran war ends
daily_entries.prediction_impact: The ultimatum deadline expiring without Iranian compliance creates a binary decision point: Trump either follows through (striking power plants, triggering full Hormuz closure and Gulf infrastructure retaliation) or backs down (damaging credibility). Either outcome is escalatory. The arrival of the Tripoli ARG with amphibious-capable Marines coincides with this decision point — Kharg Island seizure remains the most likely ground operation if escalation continues.
daily_entries.source: NBC News, USNI News, Axios, Military.com, CBS News, Defence Security Asia, Political Wire

daily_entries.summary: US-Ukraine bilateral peace talks held in Miami Mar 21-22. Witkoff and Kushner met Ukrainian delegation including Defense Minister Umerov, NSC Secretary Budanov, First Deputy Head Kyslytsia, and parliamentary leader Arakhamia. Witkoff called talks 'constructive' and said they 'focused on narrowing and resolving remaining items.' Notably, Russia was NOT present — this was bilateral only. Last trilateral talks were Geneva Feb 17-18. On the frontline, Russia advancing on Kostiantynivka and toward Kramatorsk/Sloviansk.
details: Miami talks Mar 21-22 — US-Ukraine bilateral only, no Russian delegation
Witkoff: focused on 'narrowing and resolving remaining items to move closer to a comprehensive peace agreement'
Over 8,000 Russian killed/seriously wounded in the past week per Zelensky
Russia advancing toward Hryhorivka and Prymorske near Konka river
Russian advances in central Sotnytskyi Kozachok, northwest of Kharkiv
Russian advances northeast of Orikhovo-Vasylivka near E40 highway
Russia still focused on logistical hub of Kostiantynivka (entered Dec 2025)
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Peace talks resuming bilaterally (US-Ukraine only) suggests a shift in approach — building Ukrainian consensus before bringing Russia back. However, without Russia at the table, no breakthrough is possible. The continued Russian territorial advances show the war grinds on regardless of diplomatic activity.
daily_entries.source: Kyiv Independent, Al Jazeera, EMPR Media, PBS, ABC News

daily_entries.summary: Trump-Xi summit postponed indefinitely. Originally scheduled Mar 31-Apr 2 in Beijing; Trump said delay 'a month or so' due to Iran war, later said 'reset' to mid-May. Admin now telling foreign officials summit won't happen until Iran war ends. US intelligence assesses China NOT planning Taiwan invasion in 2027. China's military closely studying US-Israeli operations against Iran for lessons applicable to Taiwan scenario per Bloomberg.
details: Trump told Financial Times China should help break Iran's Hormuz blockade
Political Wire: admin says summit 'on hold until war ends'
Bloomberg: China studying Iran war for Taiwan lessons
US intelligence: China seeks to control Taiwan without force; no 2027 invasion planned
Taiwan faced Mar 15 deadline to sign three US arms packages or lose deals
China's 2026 defense budget: ~$278B (+7%)
daily_entries.prediction_impact: China using Iran war as a live intelligence-gathering opportunity on US military capabilities is exactly the kind of strategic learning Jiang would highlight. The summit delay further demonstrates how the Iran war is consuming US diplomatic bandwidth across all theatres.
daily_entries.source: Washington Post, Bloomberg, CNBC, NBC News, Political Wire, Military Times, Al Jazeera, SCMP

daily_entries.summary: Trump issues 48-hour ultimatum: reopen Strait of Hormuz or US will 'hit and obliterate' Iran's power plants 'starting with the biggest one first.' Iran counter-threatens to target 'all energy, information technology and desalination infrastructure belonging to the US and the regime in the region.' 22 countries (UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Canada, South Korea, Australia, UAE, Bahrain, and 11 others) signed joint statement condemning Hormuz closure and signaling readiness to contribute to reopening efforts.
details: Trump posted ultimatum on Truth Social Saturday night
Iran's military response: if energy infrastructure targeted, Gulf energy/desalination/IT infrastructure will be hit
Mar 19 joint statement from 22 countries condemned attacks on commercial vessels and called on Iran to comply with UNSC Resolution 2817
Statement does not commit specific forces — signals readiness only
Brent crude at $112.19/bbl — Goldman Sachs says triple-digit oil may persist for years
Iran has refused to discuss reopening Hormuz while under attack (Bloomberg, Mar 20)
daily_entries.prediction_impact: The 48-hour ultimatum represents a dangerous escalation ladder. If Iran does not comply and Trump strikes power plants, Iran has pre-committed to retaliating against Gulf energy infrastructure — a spiral that could devastate the global energy supply far beyond what even Jiang predicted.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, CNN, CBS News, Fortune, Bloomberg, NBC News, GOV.UK

daily_entries.summary: Ground invasion indicators significantly elevated. Pentagon has drawn up detailed plans for ground operations in Iran (CBS, Times of Israel). Trump considering Kharg Island occupation or blockade (Axios, Mar 20). Additional 2,500 Marines ordered to region. USS Tripoli ARG + 31st MEU transited Malacca Strait Mar 18. Trump says he's 'not putting troops anywhere' but adds 'if I were, I certainly wouldn't tell you.'
details: Three sources told Axios that Kharg Island occupation by ground troops is 'under serious consideration'
Pentagon plans include deployment of troops into Iran per CBS sources
USS Tripoli + USS New Orleans + 31st MEU (2,200 Marines with F-35Bs) arriving end of March
Total ~4,700 additional Marines deploying to theatre
Defense experts warn of Indo-Pacific security gap as Marines deploy from Japan (Stars and Stripes)
Israel launched ground operations in southern Lebanon on Mar 16 — sets regional precedent for ground action
Still no draft legislation, no Selective Service activation, no large-scale Army deployments
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Kharg Island occupation would partially validate Jiang's prediction of US ground involvement, though it would be a limited amphibious operation (~5,000 troops) rather than the 500K-2M mainland invasion he described. The fact that ground planning is now confirmed represents a significant shift from the air-only campaign of weeks 1-2.
daily_entries.source: Axios, Military.com, CBS News, Times of Israel, Stars and Stripes, USNI News, India TV News

daily_entries.summary: Brent crude at $112.19/bbl, up from $103.14 on Mar 14. Goldman Sachs warns triple-digit oil prices may persist for years. South Pars gas field strike (Mar 18) and Iran's Ras Laffan retaliation (Mar 19) have created energy-infrastructure war spiral. Pakistan implementing four-day work week, school closures, and fuel conservation measures. Japan and South Korea releasing joint strategic petroleum reserves.
details: Oil gyrating between $100-120/bbl since war began; currently $112.19
Goldman Sachs says prices may stay in triple digits for years (CNN, Mar 20)
Pakistan: fuel queues, four-day work week, school closures, work-from-home mandates
Japan: 90% of oil via Middle East, most through Hormuz; Nikkei down 8.6%
South Korea: 70% of crude from Middle East; Kospi down 12%
India: ordered refineries to maximize LPG production, output up 25%
IEA 400M barrel strategic reserve release ongoing but markets doubt lasting impact
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Global economic impact of Hormuz blockade continuing to intensify. Jiang's thesis that Hormuz closure would be devastating to global economy is comprehensively confirmed. Energy-infrastructure targeting spiral (South Pars → Ras Laffan) escalating beyond what was predicted.
daily_entries.source: CNN, Goldman Sachs, Fortune, oilpriceapi.com, IEA, CSMonitor, CFR

daily_entries.summary: Iran war enters fourth week with no clear end in sight. NPR reports war has killed 1,444+ in Iran (including 204 children), 18 in Israel, 13 US service members. At least 74 wounded in Iranian missile strike on Arad, Israel — highest single-attack casualty count of the war. Total Israeli wounded exceeds 3,730.
details: 161 combat engagements recorded in Ukraine on same day — war continues on two fronts
Arad strike: 7 in serious condition, 74 total wounded at two impact sites
US wounded: ~200 total, 180+ returned to duty
War shows no signs of diplomatic resolution
daily_entries.source: NPR, Al Jazeera, CBS News, CNN

daily_entries.summary: Axios reports Trump mulling Kharg Island occupation to force Iran to reopen Hormuz. Military.com reports additional 2,500 Marines ordered to region. Bloomberg reports Iran unwilling to discuss Hormuz reopening while under attack.
details: Three sources told Axios Kharg occupation 'under serious consideration'
Fortune reports war has created 'private oil lane' for China and other countries playing ball with Iran
Iran continues selective passage for India, Turkey, and other 'friendly' nations
Trump told reporters: 'not putting troops anywhere' but would not 'tell you' if he were
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Kharg Island occupation planning is the closest development to Jiang's ground invasion prediction. However, island seizure with Marines is fundamentally different from the massive mainland invasion Jiang described.
daily_entries.source: Axios, Military.com, Bloomberg, Fortune

daily_entries.summary: 22 countries sign joint statement on Strait of Hormuz condemning Iran's closure and signaling readiness to contribute to reopening. Iran retaliates for South Pars strike by hitting Qatar's Ras Laffan and oil refineries in Kuwait and Saudi Arabia. Gas facilities in UAE also attacked.
details: Signatories: UK, France, Germany, Italy, Netherlands, Japan, Canada, South Korea, New Zealand, Denmark, Latvia, Slovenia, Estonia, Norway, Sweden, Finland, Czechia, Romania, Bahrain, Lithuania, Australia, UAE
Statement calls on Iran to comply with UNSC Resolution 2817
Iran's Ras Laffan attack caused 'extensive damage' per QatarEnergy
Additional Iranian attacks on oil refineries in Kuwait and Saudi Arabia Mar 19
State Department approved arms sales to Gulf countries
daily_entries.prediction_impact: The 22-country coalition forming around Hormuz reopening confirms the global strategic importance Jiang assigned to the strait. However, the coalition is diplomatic, not yet military — no country has committed warships beyond existing deployments.
daily_entries.source: GOV.UK, BusinessToday, The Hill, Al Jazeera, Euronews

daily_entries.summary: Israel struck South Pars gas field processing facilities at Asaluyeh, damaging ~12% of Iran's gas production. Israel also assassinated Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib in Tehran — third top official killed in 48 hours. USS Tripoli ARG transited Malacca Strait en route to Middle East.
details: South Pars strike: Israel hit petrochemical facilities, storage tanks, gas installations at Asaluyeh
Israel claimed coordination with US; Trump said US 'knew nothing'
Iran retaliated by striking Qatar's Ras Laffan Industrial City, causing 'extensive damage'
Khatib assassination follows Larijani (Mar 17) and Basij commander Soleimani (Mar 17)
WTI advanced 3.3% to $98.60; European gas benchmark jumped 6%
USS Tripoli + USS New Orleans + 31st MEU entered Indian Ocean via Malacca Strait
daily_entries.prediction_impact: South Pars strike marks escalation to energy-infrastructure warfare. Iran's retaliatory strike on Ras Laffan shows the spiral Jiang warned about — attacks on energy infrastructure beget more attacks on energy infrastructure. Three senior assassinations in 48 hours represents decapitation campaign unprecedented in modern warfare.
daily_entries.source: Bloomberg, CNN, NBC News, Al Jazeera, Axios, USNI News, NPR

daily_entries.summary: Israel assassinated Ali Larijani, Secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council (described as 'most powerful man in Iran' / de facto leader), along with his son Morteza and aide Alireza Bayat. Separately, Israel killed Basij militia commander Gholamreza Soleimani the same day. Two of the most significant Iranian officials killed since Khamenei.
details: Netanyahu: 'We eliminated Ali Larijani, the boss of the Revolutionary Guards'
Larijani appointed SNSC secretary by Pezeshkian in August 2025
Al Jazeera analysis: Israel killed Larijani to 'torpedo' chance of US-Iran talks
Basij commander Soleimani killed in separate strike same night
Iran vowed 'Israel will pay' for the killings
HRW report (Mar 17) documented 'unlawful strikes across Gulf endangering civilians'
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Decapitation strategy goes far beyond what Jiang predicted. Israel is systematically eliminating Iran's leadership chain — Khamenei (Feb 28), Larijani (Mar 17), Soleimani (Mar 17), Khatib (Mar 18). This suggests the war aims include regime collapse, not just denuclearization.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, Washington Post, Bloomberg, NBC News, Times of Israel, HRW

daily_entries.summary: Israel launched 'limited and targeted ground operations' in southern Lebanon against Hezbollah. IDF 91st Division deployed to towns including Kfar Kila, Houla, Kfar Shouba, Yaroun, and Khiam. Second IDF division deployed. Lebanon death toll rises to 1,001+ killed since Mar 2, with 1M+ displaced.
details: IDF stated operations aimed at establishing 'forward defense area'
Defense Minister Katz: operations continue until Hezbollah no longer threatens northern communities
UNIFIL reported Israeli soldiers entered several towns and villages
Israeli airstrike on Beirut hotel killed four IRGC Quds Force commanders
Israeli strike on health center killed 12 medical workers
Hezbollah-Iran joint missile and drone strike on Israel; Lebanon's FM summoned Iran's chargé d'affaires
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Israeli ground operations in Lebanon represent the first ground combat of the 2026 war. This is ground action against Hezbollah in Lebanon, not against Iran in Iran — an important distinction for evaluating Jiang's ground invasion prediction.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, FDD, Axios, Washington Post, Bloomberg

daily_entries.summary: Iran shifts from total blockade to selective passage at Hormuz. Two Indian LPG carriers (Shivalik, Nanda Devi — 92,712 metric tons combined) crossed safely with Indian Navy escort. Turkey also confirmed passage for a Turkish ship. IRGC navy chief says strait 'not militarily closed, merely under control.'
details: Iran's policy: ships from US, Israel, and 'their allies' barred; 'friendly' nations allowed
India's FM Jaishankar in direct talks with Iran's Araghchi on passage
Saudi oil tanker with 1M barrels destined for India also reportedly allowed through
IRGC Deputy FM: 'Countries that joined the aggression should not benefit from safe passage'
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Selective blockade is a more nuanced outcome than the total closure Jiang discussed — Iran is weaponizing access diplomatically, not just militarily.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, India TV News, Daily Sabah, Middle East Monitor

daily_entries.summary: Trump calls on China, France, Japan, South Korea, and UK to send warships to help reopen Strait of Hormuz. USS Tripoli amphibious assault ship and 2,500 Marines ordered to the region. Experts question whether the administration had a plan for Hormuz closure.
details: Three US carrier strike groups already deployed (Lincoln, Ford, G.H.W. Bush)
Trump: US will 'bomb hell out of Iran's shoreline' if attacks on strait continue
No country has yet publicly committed ships to Trump's proposed coalition
Analysts note the call exposes the gap between military capability and commercial deterrence
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Jiang predicted the US would struggle with Hormuz — the need to recruit a multilateral coalition to reopen a strait the US Navy cannot secure unilaterally for commercial traffic supports his thesis.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, Bloomberg, Fortune, Axios, Washington Examiner

daily_entries.summary: Saudi Arabia intercepted 6 Iranian ballistic missiles targeting al-Kharj and destroyed 3 drones over the Eastern Province. Qatar intercepted an incoming missile. Two killed in Oman by Iranian drones — first Omani fatalities of the war.
details: Iran previously targeted Saudi Arabia with 50 drones in a single wave (Mar 13)
Saudi had intercepted 48 drones and 31 drones/3 missiles in earlier waves
Oman had been largely spared due to its mediator role — two dead marks escalation
Saudi-Iran rapprochement under severe strain but diplomatic channels remain open
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Jiang emphasized Iran would strike across the Gulf — confirmed. Saudi-Iran rapprochement has not collapsed despite sustained attacks, contrary to expectations of immediate rupture.
daily_entries.source: Euronews, Al Jazeera, The National, IranWire

daily_entries.summary: Lebanon death toll reaches 773 killed and 1,933 wounded since Israel resumed strikes on March 2. Israel killed 12 medics at a health center in Burj Qalaouiyah. 800,000+ displaced. Israel claims it killed 5 senior IRGC Quds Force commanders in Beirut.
details: Israel has conducted ~1,100 strikes on Lebanon since Feb 28
Hezbollah firing 100+ rockets in single barrages coordinated with Iranian missile attacks
Israel threatened 'Gaza-scale devastation' and destroyed a bridge in Lebanon
UNIFIL recorded 210+ Hezbollah missiles fired into Israel since Mar 2
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, Washington Post, CNN, NPR

daily_entries.summary: North Korea fired 10+ ballistic missiles into the East Sea from the Sunan area (~350km range), its first launch since January 27. Timed to coincide with Freedom Shield US-South Korea exercises (Mar 9-19).
details: Kim Yo Jong accused US and South Korea of 'destroying the stability of East Asia'
South Korea condemned as violation of UNSC resolutions, called for immediate halt
North Korea also stepped up anti-US rhetoric in response to Iran strikes
38 North analysis: DPRK using Iran war moment to assert regional presence
daily_entries.prediction_impact: DPRK exploiting US focus on Iran to test boundaries — consistent with Jiang's broader thesis about US overextension enabling adversary action elsewhere.
daily_entries.source: Seoul Economic Daily, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, 38 North

daily_entries.summary: US-China trade chiefs (Bessent, Greer, He Lifeng) meeting in Paris this weekend to prepare agenda for Trump-Xi summit. Topics include Boeing purchases, soybeans, Taiwan, and fentanyl tariffs struck down by the Supreme Court.
details: US-Taiwan Agreement on Reciprocal Trade signed Feb 12 — Taiwan to buy $84.8B in US goods (LNG, aircraft, power equipment) through 2029
Tariffs lowered to 15% on Taiwan in exchange for near-elimination of tariffs on US goods
China condemned the deal, saying it would 'drain Taiwan's economic interests'
China has escalated drone operations against Taiwan in recent weeks
daily_entries.source: Bloomberg, PBS, SCMP, CNBC, USTR

daily_entries.summary: Maduro's next court date delayed from March 17 to March 26. Alliance for Global Justice organizing protests at federal courthouse.
details: Delay announced on Feb 17
Maduro remains detained at MDC-Brooklyn
Faces narco-terrorism conspiracy, cocaine importation conspiracy, and weapons charges
daily_entries.source: Alliance for Global Justice, DOJ filing

daily_entries.summary: Turkey confirms third Iranian ballistic missile intercepted by NATO air defenses in the Eastern Mediterranean. FM Fidan warned Iran 'next time, Turkey will respond in kind.' NATO Secretary General Rutte says Article 5 not under consideration.
details: All three missiles (Mar 4, 9, 13) reportedly aimed at Incirlik Air Base area
Iran denies intentional targeting, cites 'technical anomaly'
Turkey running diplomatic back-channel between Washington and Tehran
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Jiang predicted US would get 'trapped' in Iran — the war is expanding to hit NATO member Turkey, but Turkey is not escalating. Regional containment holding so far.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, CNBC, Washington Times

daily_entries.summary: Cuban President Diaz-Canel confirms secret negotiations with the US. Secretary of State Rubio has led at least half a dozen meetings with Cuban representatives.
details: White House says Trump believes a deal 'could be done easily'
Cuba warns agreement 'will take time'
Comes amid 90% fuel import cutoff following Venezuela operation
daily_entries.source: Washington Post, ABC News, Fox News

daily_entries.summary: Three more ships struck in Persian Gulf. Oil closes above $100 for second consecutive day at $103.14. IEA authorized record 400M barrel strategic reserve release.
details: Brent peaked at $126/bbl earlier in the week
Markets doubt reserve release will provide lasting relief
Fortune: 'Biggest oil supply disruption in history'
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Jiang predicted Hormuz blockade would be devastating — confirmed. Oil prices have not yet hit the $200 IRGC projection.
daily_entries.source: CNBC, CNN, Fortune, IEA

daily_entries.summary: IRGC struck Thailand-flagged bulk carrier MV Mayuree Naree in Strait of Hormuz. 3 crew missing, 20 rescued. At least three commercial vessels attacked on this day alone.
details: Ship was sailing in ballast from UAE to India
Two oil tankers also attacked by Iranian drone boat off Basra, Iraq — 1+ crew killed
Thailand summoned Iran's ambassador, demanded apology
IRGC claimed ship ignored 'warnings'
daily_entries.prediction_impact: War expanding to hit neutral countries' shipping — Thailand has no involvement in the conflict.
daily_entries.source: CBS News, Bloomberg, USNI News, Nation Thailand

daily_entries.summary: US formally recognized Delcy Rodriguez as 'sole' head of state of Venezuela via court filing. Opposition leader Machado welcomed the recognition.
daily_entries.source: Bloomberg, ColombiaOne, UPI

daily_entries.summary: Mojtaba Khamenei announced as new Supreme Leader of Iran, elected by Assembly of Experts under significant IRGC pressure.
details: Assembly of Experts election ran March 3-8
Mojtaba is son of assassinated Ali Khamenei
Stated Hormuz blockade must continue
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Jiang predicted Khamenei assassination — confirmed. Dynastic succession was not predicted.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, Reuters

daily_entries.summary: US and Venezuela agreed to re-establish diplomatic and consular relations, severed since 2019. Embassies to reopen.
daily_entries.source: US State Department

daily_entries.summary: By this date, Iran had launched 189 ballistic missiles, 941 drones, and 3 cruise missiles against the UAE alone. All major Gulf states reporting damage to energy infrastructure.
details: UAE ADNOC Ruwais refinery (922K bbl/day) shut down after drone strike
Dubai International Airport struck — evacuation ordered
Major maritime insurers cancelled war risk cover for Gulf transit
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Scale of Iranian retaliation across Gulf states exceeds what most analyses predicted. Jiang's framing of Iran as formidable adversary partially vindicated.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, CNBC, TIME

daily_entries.summary: QatarEnergy halted all gas production after Iranian strikes on gas facilities. IRGC declares Strait of Hormuz closed. Qatar is the world's largest LNG exporter.
details: Force majeure declared on all gas contracts March 4
Qatar PM called attacks 'a big sense of betrayal'
European natural gas prices nearly doubled
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Jiang predicted Hormuz closure — confirmed. Qatar gas halt is a major escalation with global consequences he specifically discussed.
daily_entries.source: Al Jazeera, CNBC

daily_entries.summary: US and Israel launched ~900 strikes in 12 hours on Iran. Supreme Leader Khamenei assassinated in Tehran. Iran retaliates across 9+ countries. IRGC begins Hormuz blockade.
details: At least 1,444 killed, 18,551 injured in Iran (Health Ministry figures)
Missile struck a girls' school in Minab, killing ~170
Iran responded with 500+ missiles and ~2,000 drones
daily_entries.prediction_impact: Jiang predicted US-Israel war on Iran, Khamenei assassination, and Hormuz blockade — all confirmed on this single day.
daily_entries.source: Multiple (Al Jazeera, BBC, Reuters, NPR, CNN)
